verses.text: 'Many a time have they afflicted me from my youth,' Israel says, the plowers making long furrows on her back; yet 'the LORD is righteous: he hath cut asunder the cords of the wicked.' Affliction did not destroy her. God's people are often wounded but never finally overcome.

'Many a time have they afflicted me from my youth,' Israel says, the plowers making long furrows on her back; yet 'the LORD is righteous: he hath cut asunder the cords of the wicked.' Affliction did not destroy her. God's people are often wounded but never finally overcome.

'Many a time have they afflicted me from my youth,' Israel says, the plowers making long furrows on her back; yet 'the LORD is righteous: he hath cut asunder the cords of the wicked.' Affliction did not destroy her. God's people are often wounded but never finally overcome.

'Many a time have they afflicted me from my youth,' Israel says, the plowers making long furrows on her back; yet 'the LORD is righteous: he hath cut asunder the cords of the wicked.' Affliction did not destroy her. God's people are often wounded but never finally overcome.

Let all who hate Zion be put to shame and wither like rootless grass on the housetops, ungathered and unblessed. Their malice comes to nothing. The enemies of God's people fade as quickly as they rise.
